exampleSentences: Anh có thể bốc vài miếng khoai tây chiên cho em không?
Chị thấy em bốc kẹo trên bàn mà chưa xin phép.
Mẹ dặn không nên bốc cơm bằng tay khi ăn cùng người lớn.
notes:
bốc = to pick something up with your hands, usually directly and quickly. It often implies grabbing without tools (no spoon, no chopsticks).

Common with small, loose things: food, candy, money, dirt, sand. It can feel a bit casual or even rude depending on context (especially with food).

It can also mean “to scoop up” or “to grab a handful,” not carefully but in a quick motion.

Strong memory link: opposite vibe of gắp (pick up food politely with chopsticks).

translation.en: I will pick up this paper.

example.en: I will draw lots to choose the winner.
example.vi: Tôi sẽ bốc thăm để chọn người thắng.
example.es: Sacaré un sorteo para elegir al ganador.

definitions.en: To draw or pick something, often by chance.
definitions.es: Sacar o elegir algo, a menudo por azar.
definitions.vi: Lấy hoặc chọn một cái gì đó, thường là ngẫu nhiên.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you pick up some chips for me with your hands?
I saw you pick up candy on the table without permission.
Mom reminded me not to pick up rice with my hands when eating with adults.

It can also mean “to scoop up” or “to grab a handful,” not carefully but in a quick motion.

Strong memory link: opposite vibe of **gắp** (pick up food politely with chopsticks).
